title: Laguna Art Museum
desc: The oldest art museum in Orange County, focused exclusively on California art from the plein air era to contemporary.

title: Thousand Steps Beach
desc: A secluded beach at the base of a long staircase in South Laguna, known for its dramatic cliffs and tide pools.

title: Victoria Beach
desc: A secluded beach known for its distinctive 1926 stone tower and rocky tide pool areas.

title: Pageant of the Masters
desc: A live performance where volunteers recreate famous paintings and sculptures using real people, running annually since 1933.

title: Sawdust Art Festival
desc: An open-air art festival where Laguna Beach artists sell work directly from hand-built booths, running since 1966.

title: The Cliff Restaurant
desc: A landmark oceanfront restaurant perched on the bluffs above the Pacific, serving Laguna Beach since 1946.

title: Founding and Early History
desc: How Laguna Beach grew from an indigenous homeland to an artists colony to an incorporated city.

title: The 1993 Firestorm
desc: A wildfire that destroyed 441 homes in Laguna Beach on October 27, 1993, reshaping the city's relationship with fire and development.

title: Tide Pools
desc: Laguna Beach hosts some of Southern California's most accessible and biodiverse intertidal zones.

title: Whale Watching
desc: Gray whales migrate past Laguna Beach every winter, visible from shore and by boat.

title: South Laguna
desc: The quieter southern stretch of Laguna Beach, home to hidden beaches and residential hillside neighborhoods.

title: The Village
desc: Laguna Beach's walkable downtown core, centered on Pacific Coast Highway and Forest Avenue.

title: Laguna Coast Wilderness Park
desc: A 7,000-acre protected open space surrounding Laguna Beach with 40+ miles of trails through coastal sage scrub and oak woodland.

title: Top of the World
desc: A panoramic viewpoint and trailhead offering 360-degree views from the Pacific Ocean to the Santa Ana Mountains.
